samples.xml_description:
<module id="accu">
    <description>Accumulate 4 input data and output the result.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock input for synchronization.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="data_in" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit input data for addition.</input>
        <input id="valid_in">Input signal indicating readiness for new data.</input>
        <output id="valid_out">Output signal indicating when 4 input data accumulation is reached.</output>
        <output id="data_out" width_description="[9:0]">10-bit output data representing the accumulated sum.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <enum id="state" width_description="[1:0]">
            <enum-value id="idle" value="2'b00">Idle state, waiting for valid_in.</enum-value>
            <enum-value id="accumulate" value="2'b01">Accumulate state, accumulating input data.</enum-value>
            <enum-value id="output" value="2'b10">Output state, outputting the accumulated result.</enum-value>
        </enum>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg id="state_reg" type="sequential_logic" width_description="[1:0]">Current state of the FSM.</reg>
        <reg id="count_reg" type="sequential_logic" width_description="[1:0]">Count of input data received.</reg>
        <reg id="data_out_reg" type="sequential_logic" width_description="[9:0]">Register to store the accumulated result.</reg>
        <reg id="valid_out_reg" type="sequential_logic">Register to store the valid_out signal.</reg>
        <reg id="sum_reg" type="sequential_logic" width_description="[9:0]">Register to store the sum of input data.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        The module operates in three states: idle, accumulate, and output. 
        In the idle state, it waits for valid_in to be asserted. 
        In the accumulate state, it accumulates input data until 4 values are received. 
        In the output state, it outputs the accumulated result and sets valid_out to 1. 
        The process repeats until valid_in is asserted again.
    </implementation>
</module>
